description_long: This is a classic pipe wrench featuring a robust metal head with an adjustable jaw mechanism and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head is dark, likely made of cast iron or steel, and includes a knurled adjustment nut for altering the jaw width. The handle is made of wood, stained a medium brown, and capped with a metallic ferrule at the end for durability. The wrench's jaw is serrated, designed to grip pipes and rounded objects securely. The adjustment mechanism is visible and appears functional, with a blue-painted shaft connecting the head to the handle. The overall appearance suggests a well-used, utilitarian tool suitable for plumbing or mechanical work in urban environments.
description: A sturdy pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head, serrated jaws, and a long brown wooden handle with a blue shaft. The tool shows signs of use and is built for gripping and turning pipes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded end of the jaw and the adjustment nut, with the handle receding backward.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the wrench, the serrated jaw profile, adjustment nut, blue shaft, and wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the rounded wooden handle end and the back of the adjustment mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the wrench, including the jaw, adjustment nut, and handle.
attribute_handle_grip_pattern: smooth wood with visible grain, no distinct grip pattern
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: minor wear marks on the metal head and adjustment nut, no significant rust visible
